title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Poirot follows Jerome to Mrs. Habert's room, where she provides him with a written timeline of recent events. Mrs. Habert explains that Mrs. Nicolaitis was upset by a police search that resulted in the discovery of numerous empty brandy bottles after an officer broke down her cabinet door. 

While reviewing the timeline, Poirot notes that a canvas bag was listed as the first incident, occurring before the jewelry thefts. Mrs. Habert recalls that the bag belonged to Lennon Bates, which was eventually found behind the boiler cut into pieces. This incident coincided with the police questioning an African student regarding "immoral income," an event that also occurred on the same day a lightbulb went missing from the public lounge. Jerome had previously reported that all spare bulbs were missing, forcing the use of candles.

Poirot investigates the nature of the damaged property, viewing a backpack in Colin MacNab's room that is nearly identical to Lennon's destroyed bag. He observes that the bag would require significant strength and malice to cut, rather than simple scissors. Mrs. Habert mentions that Valerie's scarf was also found cut into pieces. 

After discussing the timeline of thefts, which began around late February, Poirot examines the view from the balcony. He identifies the location of the boiler room near the coal shed and notes the layout of the house, observing that Colin MacNab's room, Nigel Chapman's room, and Lennon Bates' rooms face the back garden, while the girls' rooms are located nearby.
